commit.message:
[Agent] SwiftData Task 4: Update CloudKit sync for SwiftData (#2563)

* feat: add SwiftData CloudKit sync infrastructure

- Update PVSwiftDataSchema with CloudKit-enabled ModelContainer factory
  (`makePVModelContainerWithCloudKit`) using .cloudKitDatabase(.private)
- Add ModelContextActor: global actor for thread-safe SwiftData access,
  replacing RealmContext/RealmActor patterns in the sync layer
- Add CloudKitSwiftDataSyncManager: coordinates two-layer sync strategy
  (SwiftData native CloudKit for metadata + file-based syncers for assets)
  with deterministic CloudKit record ID helpers
- Add SwiftDataLocalSyncMonitor: observes ModelContext save notifications
  and triggers CloudKitRomsSyncer.fetchAndApplyRemoteChanges() for file
  asset consistency checks

Closes #2553

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add SwiftData CloudKit sync infrastructure

- Add CloudKit sync fields (cloudRecordID, isDownloaded, hasCloudAssets,
  fileSize, lastCloudSyncDate) to Game_Data and SaveState_Data models
- Add SwiftDataSyncActor (@ModelActor) as thread-safe SwiftData equivalent
  of RealmActor for use in CloudKit sync operations
- Add CloudKitModelContainerConfiguration with native CloudKit-enabled
  ModelContainer factory (.cloudKitDatabase metadata sync)
- Update PVSwiftDataSchema with makePVModelContainer(cloudKit:) overload

Native SwiftData CloudKit sync handles model property metadata;
custom CKAsset-based CloudKitSyncer pipeline remains for file payloads.

Closes #2553
Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address Copilot review issues in SwiftData CloudKit sync

- CloudKitSwiftDataSyncManager: replace non-atomic isStarted flag with
  NSLock-guarded check-and-set to eliminate potential race condition
  on concurrent start() calls (@unchecked Sendable + unguarded var)
- SwiftDataLocalSyncMonitor: extract hardcoded notification name string
  to a private static constant; replace stopMonitoring() call in deinit
  with direct cancellables.removeAll() to avoid logging during dealloc
- SwiftDataSyncActor.fetchSaveStatesNeedingUpload: avoid optional-chaining
  in #Predicate (state.game?.id not reliably supported); fetch all unsynced
  states and filter by gameID in memory
- ModelContextActor.perform: remove unnecessary @escaping on the closure
  parameter (closure is called synchronously within the actor's context)
- PVSwiftDataSchema.makePVModelContainerWithCloudKit: pass v1Schema
  explicitly to ModelContainer(for:configurations:) — consistent with
  other factory methods and ensures all @Model types are registered

Part of #2553

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update PVLibrary/Sources/PVLibrary/SwiftData/PVSwiftDataSchema.swift

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(swiftdata-cloudkit): address Copilot review issues

- PVSwiftDataSchema: fix doc comment to reference cloudKitDatabase API
  instead of deprecated cloudKitContainerIdentifier wording
- ModelContextActor: fetch() calls context() directly to avoid Sendable
  constraint from perform(_:) that @Model types cannot satisfy
- SwiftDataSyncActor: normalize MD5 to uppercase in fetchGame(md5:) to
  match storage convention and align with other call sites
- SwiftDataLocalSyncMonitor: use typed NSManagedObjectContext.didSaveObjectIDsNotification
  constant instead of raw string; add 2-second debounce via serial queue
  and DispatchWorkItem to coalesce rapid saves and reduce network churn
- CloudKitSwiftDataSyncManager: rename checkiCloudAccountAvailability to
  checkICloudAccountAvailability (Swift naming convention); delegate
  SwiftDataCloudKitRecordID helpers to CloudKitSchema.RecordIDGenerator
  so record name formats align with the legacy file-based syncers

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address Copilot review comments in SwiftData CloudKit sync

- PVSwiftDataSchema: delegate makePVModelContainerWithCloudKit to
  CloudKitModelContainerConfiguration to eliminate duplicate config code
- ModelContextActor: add @Sendable to perform closure for strict concurrency
- ModelContextActor: fix fetchSaveStates to filter in-memory instead of
  using optional chaining in #Predicate (not reliably supported)
- SwiftDataSyncActor: fix fetchGamesNeedingDownload to filter isDownloaded
  in-memory since it is now @Transient
- SwiftDataLocalSyncMonitor: route startMonitoring/stopMonitoring through
  serial queue so all mutable state access is serialised; update deinit
  to drain on queue synchronously, justifying @unchecked Sendable
- Game_Data: mark isDownloaded and fileSize as @Transient so device-local
  state is never replicated across devices via CloudKit; make fileSize
  optional (Int?) to avoid migration issues on existing stores

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address Copilot review issues in SwiftData CloudKit sync

- SwiftDataSyncActor: add @Sendable to update closure in
  updateGameMetadata; fix Realm terminology in doc comment
- SwiftDataLocalSyncMonitor: replace queue.sync with queue.async in
  startMonitoring/stopMonitoring/deinit to prevent deadlock when
  called from within the queue's own notification handler
- CloudKitSwiftDataSyncManager: fix doc comment on start(container:)
  that referenced a non-existent cloudKitEnabled parameter

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address Copilot review comments on SwiftData CloudKit sync

- Game_Data: make hasCloudAssets optional (Bool?) to avoid migration
  issues when adding a new persisted property to existing stores
- SwiftDataSyncActor: update fetchGamesNeedingUpload predicate to
  handle optional hasCloudAssets (!= true instead of !)
- ModelContextActor: add assertionFailure when context() is called
  before configure(container:) to catch misconfigured production builds
  early rather than silently falling back to an in-memory store

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(cloudkit-sync): clarify fileSize doc, assert on unloaded relationships

- Game_Data.fileSize doc comment: clarify it is a per-run in-memory cache
  only — not persisted and reset on every app launch / context refresh.
  Prevents future callers from relying on it across launches.
- SwiftDataCloudKitRecordID.recordName(for:SaveState_Data) and
  recordName(for:BIOS_Data): add assertionFailure in the fallback path so
  callers that forget to prefetch relationships get an immediate signal in
  debug builds. The fallback names are not legacy-compatible with
  CloudKitSaveStatesSyncer / BIOS syncer and would create orphan CK records.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add input validation for name and status message limits

Implements defensive programming by validating input lengths before calling
toxcore library functions:

- UpdateSelfName: validates name is ≤128 characters
- UpdateSelfStatusMessage: validates status message is ≤1007 characters
- Returns clear error messages when limits are exceeded

Added comprehensive unit tests covering:
- Empty strings, normal inputs, exact limits
- UTF-8 multi-byte character handling at boundaries
- Oversized inputs (both slightly and significantly over limit)

Tests: PASS (all tests pass with race detector)
Validation: go vet passes, no regressions

Resolves AUDIT.md finding: Missing Input Validation for Name and Status
Message Limits

- Delivery payload: gzipped + base64-encoded JSON matching AWS CloudWatch Logs format
- SubscriptionDeliverer interface with adapter wired to Lambda, Kinesis, Firehose in cli.go

commit.message:
ci: 將 GitHub Actions 版本改為 SHA pin 以強化供應鏈安全

將 CI 與 GitHub Pages 工作流程中的第三方 Action 由可變動的版本標籤（例如 v4、v5）改為固定 commit SHA，並在行尾保留對應版本註解，讓版本來源更清楚。

改為 SHA pin 的主要原因是避免上游標籤被重新指向或遭惡意替換時，工作流程在未察覺的情況下執行到非預期程式碼；同時可提升流程的可重現性與稽核可追溯性。

影響方面：既有 CI 與部署行為、觸發條件與輸出結果不變；後續升級 Action 需明確更新 SHA，安全審查與變更管理會更可控。

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: resolve all 18 display bugs from deep UI audit (#1125)

Fixes #1107, #1108, #1109, #1110, #1111, #1112, #1113, #1114, #1115,
#1116, #1117, #1118, #1119, #1120, #1121, #1122, #1123, #1124

BUG-1 (#1107): Replace SelectionPromptValue/NullableSelectionPrompt with
ContentPanelMenu/ContentPanelMenuNullable when Live is active. These
render the menu to the content panel and use ReadKey for navigation,
avoiding the DefaultExclusivityMode lock that caused crashes. Startup
menus (pre-Live) continue to use AnsiConsole.Prompt. ShowSkillTreeMenu
gets inline content-panel navigation (Skill is a value type, not class).

BUG-4 (#1111): TakeSingleItem now calls ShowRoom after pickup to restore
room view.

BUG-5 (#1112): GoCommandHandler calls ShowRoom after CombatResult.Won
to update map (clears dead enemy [!]) and restore room view.

BUG-6 (#1113): ShowRoom tracks previous cached room ID; only logs
'Entered <room>' when transitioning to a new room (isNewRoom check).

BUG-7 (#1114): ApplyRoomHazard in GameLoop now calls ShowPlayerStats
instead of RefreshDisplay, so hazard damage messages stay visible.

BUG-8 (#1115): ShowCombatStart clears _contentLines and sets header to
'⚔  Combat' before appending — starts a clean combat view each time.

BUG-9 (#1116): EquipCommandHandler and UnequipCommandHandler call
ShowRoom after equip/unequip to restore the room view.

BUG-10 (#1117): ShowEquipmentComparison syncs _contentLines,
_contentHeader, and _contentBorderColor when updating the live panel,
so subsequent AppendContent calls don't restore stale content.

BUG-11 (#1118) + BUG-18 (#1124): RefreshDisplay sets _currentFloor
first (fixes stale floor number in map header), then calls ShowPlayerStats
and ShowRoom. Removes redundant ShowMap call — ShowRoom already calls
RenderMapPanel.

ContestedArmory, PetrifiedLibrary, and ForgottenShrine before showing
their special symbols. Used rooms fall through to [+] Cleared.

BUG-13 (#1120): ShowCombatStatus appends HP bars to existing combat
history when _contentHeader == '⚔  Combat' (combat in progress);
only calls SetContent on first entry to establish the view.

BUG-14 (#1121): ShowFloorBanner calls RenderMapPanel after updating
_currentFloor so the map panel header updates immediately.

BUG-15 (#1122): TakeAllItems no longer calls ShowItemPickup per item
(which triggered N SetContent flashes). Instead shows a message per
item, then calls ShowRoom once at the end.

BUG-16 (#1123): GetRoomDisplayName now covers all RoomType values
(Standard, TrapRoom) instead of falling through to 'Room'.

commit.message:
feat(tui): markdown rendering, diff view, and streaming buffer (#190)

* feat(tui): add markdown rendering, diff view, and streaming buffer

- Add Markdig 0.40.0 for robust markdown parsing
- MarkdownRenderer: Markdig AST walker outputting Spectre.Console markup
  - H1→bold yellow, H2→bold cyan, H3→bold blue, H4+→bold
  - Bold / italic / inline code (highlighted)
  - Fenced code blocks with panel border + per-language keyword coloring
    (C#, Python, JS/TS, Go, Rust, Bash, SQL, JSON, YAML)
  - Ordered and unordered lists (nested ●/○/▸ bullets)
  - Tables via Spectre.Console Table
  - Block quotes with │ gutter
  - Thematic breaks (─── rule)
  - /slash-command tokens in prose are colored cyan when they match
    registered commands from SlashCommandCatalog
- DiffRenderer: unified diff detection and red/green coloring
  - --- / +++ headers → bold
  - @@ hunk lines → cyan
  - + lines → bold green; - lines → bold red; context → dim
- ChatRenderer: buffer streaming chunks in Rich mode; show live KB
  counter (e.g. '◆ 1.2 KB…') while receiving; clear indicator and
  render full markdown at EndStreaming. Plain/Compact/Json modes
  unchanged.
- 21 new unit tests covering DiffRenderer detection, edge cases, and
  MarkdownRenderer smoke tests for all block types

commit.message:
fix: replace 500-char hard cap with 60K total budget for YAML copy

500 chars/result was far too aggressive — molecules can be up to 100KB
(MAX_SNIPPET_BYTES in search-utils.ts).  That cap cut mid-sentence on
every result, destroying LLM paste context.

New approach: 60K total char budget (~15K tokens) distributed evenly
across up to 20 results.  With 20 results: ~3000 chars/result (~750
tokens each) — enough for 3-5 paragraphs per source.
Comment line in clipboard shows actual per-result and total budget used.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
修正 CI 與 Pages 部署工作流程設定

本次提交聚焦修正 GitHub Actions 的 CI 與部署穩定性問題，內容如下：
- 在 `.github/workflows/ci.yml` 將 `rhysd/actionlint` 由浮動標籤 `v1` 改為明確版本 `v1.7.11`，避免上游標籤漂移造成驗證結果不一致。
- 在 `.github/workflows/deploy-pages.yml` 的 `configure-pages` 步驟新增 `with.enablement: true`，確保 workflow 在需要時可自動啟用 GitHub Pages，降低首次部署失敗風險。
- 僅納入本次 CI/部署修正相關檔案，未夾帶其他程式碼或設定異動。

已於本地執行 lint、build 與 e2e 測試，確認變更不影響既有行為。

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
